coupling.note: Four new coupling edges. The coupling-auditor identified a HIGH-severity concern: Fix-A's mtimes-in-key approach forces cache-key.ts to import AppFileSystem — a new dependency from cache infrastructure to I/O layer. Recommended fix: deferred freshness check (mtimes verified post-hit from cached metadata.file_paths). Other edges: (1) file_paths convention is implicit (Metadata = [key:string]:any) with zero type enforcement — tool authors can forget file_paths and get silent stale cache; (2) write tool invalidation can be placed in pipeline (low coupling, 1 file) rather than in-tool (high coupling, 3+ files); (3) truncation reorder is a call-site relocation with same dependency. ToolCache.serviceOption keeps interface clean. Existing any-casts in wrap() hide cache dependency from type checker.

debuggability.note: BLOCKING. The debuggability-forecaster identified 5 gaps: (Gap 1) serviceOption=None produces no span — developer cannot tell 'cache disabled' from 'cache miss'; (Gap 2) incomplete cache keys (missing file_paths) produce undifferentiated misses — no 'cache.miss_reason' or 'cache.key_kind' attributes; (Gap 3) truncation failures on cache hits look identical to fresh execution failures — no 'truncation.input_source'; (Gap 4) cache hits silently skip ctx.ask() permission dialogs with zero span documentation; (Gap 5) write-side invalidation has complete span blindness — neither invalidate() nor invalidateKey() have any span annotations. All 5 gaps have specific 1-3 line fixes in cache.ts and tool.ts. Additionally: cache stats not annotated at span close (Bonus Gap 6, nice-to-have).

convergence.note: Directly solves root problem by wiring already-built getOrCompute into the wrap() hot path. Convergence-checker analyzed 7 edge cases: permissions (MITIGATED — must add session/agent to key, COND-004), abort (HANDLED — no in-flight work on cache hit), mtime preservation (MITIGATED — 5-min TTL safety net), truncation config (HANDLED — re-applied on retrieval), agent context (MITIGATED — all current cacheable tools are deterministic of args+files), partial writes (MITIGATED — affects multi-file writers only), concurrent write+read (MITIGATED — atomic Ref, bounded window). BLOCKING: read.ts is cacheable:true but produces LSP-dependent output — the LSP symbol context changes independently of file contents. Either remove cacheable:true from read.ts or add LSP server state to cache key.

surface_area.note: Surface-area-mapper counted 14 files: 1 NEW (cache-key.ts, ~30 lines), 13 MOD. Fix-B: cache.ts @deprecated removal (1 file, -3 lines). Fix-C: tool.ts wrap() (1 file, ~25-30 lines). Fix-D: 4 write tools (write, edit, shell — ~2 lines each). Fix-E: 8 read tools (read, grep, glob, read-source, read-artifact, read-lib, rig-jsonl-query, rig-schema-validate — ~2 lines each). No type signature changes: Def unchanged, ExecuteResult unchanged (file_paths flows through Metadata [key:string]:any), ToolCache.Service already in AppLayer. Zero changes to registry.ts, app-runtime.ts, session/tools.ts, or message-v2.ts. All changes are non-overlapping within files — safe for parallel execution. No fragmentation or consolidation needed.

testability.note: BLOCKING. Isolation-tester evaluated 16 test cases. 3 of 8 plan tests are TESTABLE (cache hit, miss, TTL expiry with TestClock). 5 need layer setup. 8 missing tests identified: concurrent dedup (ALREADY TESTED in cache.test.ts — 6 tests, 10-fiber stress), permission interaction (HARD), abort interaction (NEEDS_LAYER), partial write failure-never-invalidate (NEEDS_LAYER), maxEntrySize (TESTABLE — easy win), cacheable=false bypass (NEEDS_LAYER), per-tool config (NEEDS_LAYER), invalidation races (TESTABLE). The existing cache.test.ts concurrency suite is excellent and should be celebrated. Must add: maxEntrySize bypass test, permission bypass test, and abort interaction test.

error_clarity.note: Good: cache.ts defects propagate correctly via Effect.exit→Deferred.done→Effect.failCause. Write invalidation is infallible. Cache key creation can't fail. Concerns: (1) Effect.orDie at wrap() line ~232 means cache-key module errors become cryptic process crashes — add Effect.tapDefect before orDie; (2) caller span always says 'Tool.execute' — can't distinguish 'defect in cached compute' vs 'direct execute'; (3) truncation failure after cache hit indistinguishable from fresh execution failure. All 3 have specific fixes.

reversibility.note: Groups decouple cleanly. Dependency chain: A (cache-key + @deprecated) ← B (wrap() wiring) ← C (invalidation). Group D (file_paths) is a detached leaf independent of B/C. Group A alone: system builds, cache-key.ts is unused but harmless. Group B reverted: cache-key.ts orphaned, cache.ts getOrCompute dead again, write tools still invalidate (no-op if B reverted). Group C reverted: cache operational but stale after writes — wrong behavior but system builds. Group D reverted: file_paths missing from metadata — cache keys lose mtime component → staleness window enlarges. Full-cache-clear is correct but wasteful — per-key invalidation upgrade path exists via file_paths. file_paths added when cache not wired: system builds and runs correctly with no behavior change.

objections.evidence: tool.ts wrap() — serviceOption None branch has zero span annotation.
objections.finding: When ToolCache service is absent, the tool silently runs execute() directly with no span attribute. Must add Effect.annotateCurrentSpan({ 'cache.enabled': false }) in the onNone branch.

objections.evidence: cache-key.ts + cache.ts miss path — no miss_reason or key_kind span attributes.
objections.finding: Incomplete cache keys produce hits/misses indistinguishable from normal. Must add: 'cache.key_kind': 'mtime_keyed'|'constant_key' at key construction; 'cache.miss_reason': 'first_access'|'no_file_paths' at miss site; 'cache.result_source': 'cache'|'computation'|'dedup_await' in getOrCompute.

objections.evidence: cache.ts invalidate() and invalidateKey() — zero span annotations.
objections.finding: Complete blind spot for invalidation. Must add: 'cache.invalidated':true, 'cache.invalidated_scope':'full'|'key', 'cache.invalidated_entries':count, 'cache.invalidated_bytes':bytes in invalidate(); 'cache.invalidated_key':key in invalidateKey(); 'cache.invalidated_by_tool':toolId at each write tool call site.

objections.evidence: cache-key.ts key derivation lacks session and agent isolation. glob.ts:33, grep.ts:46, read.ts:229 call ctx.ask() inside execute(). On cache hit, execute() skipped entirely.
objections.finding: Cross-agent, cross-session cache contamination. Agent B can receive Agent A's cached result without its own permission check. Must add ctx.sessionID and ctx.agent to cache key via cache-key.ts.

objections.evidence: Zero pipeline-level test coverage for caching. Existing cache.test.ts has excellent concurrency tests but tests only the cache layer, not the wiring.
objections.finding: Must add pipeline-level tests: (a) maxEntrySize bypass (TESTABLE, ~7 lines — easy win), (b) permission interaction (NEEDS_LAYER), (c) abort interaction (NEEDS_LAYER). Also: existing test coverage is in packages/opencode/test/tool/cache.test.ts (6 tests, 217 lines).

objections.evidence: packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts — ReadTool marked cacheable:true. Executes LSP.Service for symbol context. LSP server state changes independently of file contents.
objections.finding: read.ts produces LSP-dependent output — caching produces stale LSP data. Either remove cacheable:true from ReadTool, or add LSP server state to cache key, or document staleness window explicitly.

objections.evidence: Fix-A: mtimes-in-key forces cache-key.ts to import AppFileSystem for stat() calls — a new dependency from cache infrastructure to I/O layer.
objections.finding: Prefer deferred freshness check: cache key = SHA256(tool_id, args) only. On cache hit, extract file_paths from cached metadata, stat those files, compare mtimes. This isolates the AppFileSystem dependency to the freshness check in wrap() rather than forcing it into the cache key module. Lower coupling, same correctness.

conditions.condition: COND-001: Add 'cache.enabled' span on serviceOption None
conditions.detail: In tool.ts wrap(), the serviceOption onNone branch: add Effect.annotateCurrentSpan({ 'cache.enabled': false }). This ensures every tool execution span shows whether caching was active.

conditions.condition: COND-002: Add miss_reason, key_kind, and result_source span attributes
conditions.detail: (a) At key construction in tool.ts: annotate 'cache.key_kind': 'mtime_keyed' | 'constant_key'. (b) When file_paths missing and tool is cacheable: annotate 'cache.miss_reason': 'no_file_paths'. (c) In cache.ts hit path: add 'cache.result_source': 'cache'. In compute path: 'cache.result_source': 'computation'. In dedup path: 'cache.result_source': 'dedup_await'.

(a) cache.ts invalidate(): capture pre-invalidation stats, annotate 'cache.invalidated':true, 'scope':'full', 'entries':count, 'bytes':bytes. (b) cache.ts invalidateKey(): annotate 'cache.invalidated':true, 'scope':'key', 'key':key. (c) Each write tool invalidation site: annotate 'cache.invalidated_by_tool':toolId, 'reason':'write_tool'.

conditions.condition: COND-004: Add sessionID and agent to cache key
conditions.detail: Modify cache-key.ts deriveCacheKey signature to include sessionID: string, agent: string. Key format: SHA256(tool_id | sorted_args_json | session_id | agent | file_mtimes_sorted). This prevents cross-agent and cross-session cache contamination.

conditions.condition: COND-005: Add at minimum 3 pipeline-level tests
conditions.detail: (a) maxEntrySize bypass — cached result exceeds size → not stored, still returned (TESTABLE, ~7 lines). (b) Permission bypass on cache hit — second same call skips ctx.ask (NEEDS_LAYER, ~15 lines). (c) Abort signal interaction — cache hit returns immediately when aborted (NEEDS_LAYER, ~15 lines). Use existing cache.test.ts patterns.

ReadTool calls LSP.Service for symbol context — output depends on live server state. Either: (a) remove cacheable:true from ReadTool, or (b) add LSP server state hash to cache key for read tool specifically, or (c) document that read.ts cached results may have stale LSP sections, with the 5-min TTL as safety net. Recommendation: (c) with explicit documentation, since the LSP data is supplementary and the file content is primary.

conditions.condition: COND-007: Prefer deferred freshness over mtimes-in-key
conditions.detail: Per coupling-auditor FIND-007: cache key should be SHA256(tool_id, args) only. On cache hit, extract file_paths from cached metadata, stat files, compare mtimes fresh. If mtimes differ, treat as miss. This keeps AppFileSystem dependency in wrap() (already depends on it indirectly) rather than in the cache infrastructure.

In tool.ts wrap() before Effect.orDie: pipe through Effect.tapDefect that logs defect with { 'tool.name': id, 'cache.in_flight': true/false }. This distinguishes cache-path defects from direct-execution defects in crash logs.

follow_up_issues.detail: Schema-decoded objects may have non-deterministic key ordering in JSON.stringify. Use sorted-keys replacer or Schema.encode for deterministic serialization. Otherwise, equivalent args produce different cache keys.

Full-cache-clear on writes is correct but wasteful. The file_paths convention enables future per-key invalidation: on write to file A, call invalidateKey for each cache key whose cached metadata.file_paths includes A. Can be added later without changing write tool interface.

follow_up_issues.issue: Metadata type safety — promote file_paths to first-class field
follow_up_issues.detail: Metadata = { [key: string]: any } makes file_paths unenforceable. Add file_paths?: string[] to ExecuteResult type alongside title, output, metadata so TypeScript enforces it for cacheable tools.

Add JSDoc to Def.cacheable: 'Cacheable tools must produce deterministic output from (parameters | filesystem state). Tools with network calls, external services, LSP queries, or agent-dependent behavior must not be cacheable.'

Document in cache.ts Interface JSDoc and tool.ts Def.cacheable that cache hits skip execute() entirely, which means ctx.ask() is never called. Span attribute 'cache.permission_bypassed': true should be present on every cache hit.
Consider annotating cache.stats() at Tool.execute span close: size, total hits/misses/evictions. Gives per-invocation context on cache health without extra query.

follow_up_issues.issue: Write tool invalidation placement — pipeline vs in-tool
follow_up_issues.detail: Coupling-auditor recommends pipeline interception (in wrap(), after successful execution) over in-tool calls. This keeps invalidation in one file and avoids touching every write tool. Use !toolInfo.cacheable as the discriminator: cacheable=false means mutation tool → invalidate.

key_findings: Permissions bypass on cache hit (ctx.ask skipped)
All 7 edge cases handled or mitigated
Specific file:line fix locations for every gap
3/8 plan tests are TESTABLE, 5 need layers
Existing cache.test.ts concurrency suite is excellent
HIGH: mtimes-in-key forces AppFileSystem import in cache infrastructure
Write invalidation: pipeline vs in-tool tradeoff (pipeline = 1 file, low coupling)
file_paths is implicit coupling (no type enforcement)
any-casts hide ToolCache dependency from type checker
14 files total (1 new, 13 mod, ~75-85 net new lines)
3 write tools need invalidation (write, edit, shell)
